section.heading: Action for infringement of a patent containing an invalid claim

content.p: Whenever a claim of a patent is invalid, an action may be maintained for the infringement of a claim of the patent which may be valid. The patentee shall recover no costs unless a disclaimer of the invalid claim has been entered at the Patent and Trademark Office before the commencement of the suit.

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: The necessity for a disclaimer to recover on valid claims is eliminated. See section 253.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2011—Pub. L. 112–29 struck out “, without deceptive intention,” after “Whenever”.
1975—Pub. L. 93–596 substituted “Patent and Trademark Office” for “Patent Office”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2011 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 112–29 effective upon the expiration of the 1-year period beginning on Sept. 16, 2011, and applicable to proceedings commenced on or after that effective date, see section 20(l) of Pub. L. 112–29, set out as a note under section 2 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1975 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 93–596 effective Jan. 2, 1975, see section 4 of Pub. L. 93–596, set out as a note under section 1111 of Title 15, Commerce and Trade.
